title: The Great Divide: Past vs. Present Perfect
description: Learning when to use simple past (finished) or present perfect (unfinished/connected).
lesson_content_markdown:
# The Great Divide: Past vs. Present Perfect

Many students find this choice difficult, but there is one simple secret: **Check the time.**

### 1. Simple Past (Finished Past)
Use this when you say exactly **when** something happened. The time is "closed."

- **Keywords**: yesterday, last week, in 2022, two hours ago, when I was a child.
- **Example**: I **saw** the professor **yesterday**. (I can't change yesterday!)
- **Example**: We **finished** the test **at 2 PM**.

### 2. Present Perfect (Unfinished/Connected Past)
Use this when the time is not finished, or you don't say exactly when it happened. The "bridge" is still open to the present.

- **Keywords**: since, for, ever, never, already, yet, this week.
- **Example**: I **have seen** the professor **three times this week**. (The week is not over!)
- **Example**: She **has worked** at the university **since 2010**.

### 3. The Comparison
- **Past**: I **ate** lunch at 12:00. (Specific time, finished.)
- **Present Perfect**: I **have already eaten** lunch. (Finished, but connected to my energy now!)
- **Past**: They **lived** in London in 2015. (They don't live there now.)
- **Present Perfect**: They **have lived** in London for 10 years. (They still live there!)

---

mastery_check.prompt_to_student: Can you handle the Great Divide? Choose carefully between the finished past and the connected present.

questions.question: I ___ the university president yesterday at the ceremony.
She ___ this science book for three hours now.
We ___ our final presentation last Monday.
___ you ever visited the campus research center?
They ___ to the new gym three times this week.
In 2021, the university ___ a new science building.
I ___ my student ID card five minutes ago.
He ___ for that company since he graduated.
Which sentence is correct for yesterday?
options: I went to the library yesterday.
I have gone to the library yesterday.
I have been to the library yesterday.
I go to the library yesterday.
questions.correct_answer: I went to the library yesterday.

questions.question: Which sentence is correct for life experience?
options: I have eaten at that cafe many times.
I ate at that cafe many times yesterday.
I have eat at that cafe before.
I already eat at that cafe.
questions.correct_answer: I have eaten at that cafe many times.

questions.question: I ___ (study) for five hours yesterday.
I ___ (study) for five hours today. I am tired!
She ___ (already / write) her English report.
They ___ (buy) a new computer last year.
They ___ (buy) three new computers since 2022.
___ you (see) the new professor this morning?
We ___ (not be) to the campus museum yet.
The sun ___ (rise) at 6:00 AM today.
He ___ (be) a doctor for ten years now.
I ___ (lose) my pen on the bus yesterday.
I *have seen* her at the party last night.
He *has lived* here since three years.
We *have finished* the work two hours ago.
She *never saw* a real elephant before today.
Did you ever *visited* the national park?
I *have ate* lunch at 12:00 yesterday.
They *lived* here since January.
Wait! I *have forgot* my keys in the car.
The university *has opened* in 1965.
I *already finished* my homework this morning.
